title: lepidagathis staurogynoides
intro: Lepidagathis staurogynoides, also known as the 'Honeycomb Bush,' is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to tropical regions of Central and South America, where it grows as a shrub with white or yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce fever. Lepidagathis staurogynoides is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
